originalAlarm.description: Value stored to 'x' during its initialization is never read

reasoning: 30e053248da.c L31: 'int x = *length' dead store. x is assigned the dereferenced value of length but x is never subsequently read (function returns 0 at L33). Genuine dead store. PC=true correctly captures that the function has no #ifdef guards — reachable in all configurations when error=0. New bug (the // (6) ERROR at L31 refers to the uninitialized *length read, not the dead store of x). Consistent with TRANSFORMATION_142 (TRUE_NEW).
